items.docs: Creates a new Spark cluster. This method acquires new instances from the cloud provider and starts the Spark driver and worker processes. The cluster is created asynchronously; use the cluster_id returned to poll for status.

params.name: can_use_client
params.description: Filter clusters by client compatibility.

items.docs: Returns information about all clusters in the workspace, including terminated clusters. Clusters are ordered by cluster_id.

params.name: cluster_id
params.description: The unique identifier of the cluster.

items.docs: Retrieves detailed information about a cluster, including its current state, configuration, and runtime properties.
Starts a terminated cluster given its cluster_id. This is similar to creating a cluster except it uses the configuration of the previously terminated cluster.
Restarts a Spark cluster given its cluster_id. If the cluster is not in a RUNNING state, nothing happens.
Terminates a Spark cluster given its cluster_id. The cluster is removed after being terminated. Use the permanent-delete endpoint if you want to remove the cluster configuration entirely.

Permanently deletes a Spark cluster. If the cluster is running, it is terminated and resources are asynchronously removed. If the cluster is terminated, it is immediately removed. A cluster can only be permanently deleted by an admin or the cluster creator.
Edits the configuration of a cluster to match the provided attributes. The cluster must be in a RUNNING or TERMINATED state. If the cluster is running, it will be restarted to apply the changes.
Retrieves a list of events about the activity of a cluster. Events are returned in reverse chronological order. This endpoint can be used to audit cluster activity and monitor lifecycle changes.
Creates a new job with the provided settings. The job can be configured with a single task or multiple tasks with dependencies forming a directed acyclic graph (DAG).

params.name: limit
params.description: The number of jobs to return. This value must be greater than 0 and less than or equal to 25. The default value is 20.

params.name: offset
params.description: The offset of the first job to return.

params.name: name
params.description: A filter on the list based on the exact (case-insensitive) job name.

params.name: expand_tasks
params.description: Whether to include task and cluster details in the response.

items.docs: Retrieves a list of jobs in the workspace. Results are paginated and can be filtered by name.

params.name: job_id
params.description: The canonical identifier of the job to retrieve.

items.docs: Retrieves the details of a single job, including its settings, creator, and run history summary.
Add, update, or remove specific settings of an existing job. Use reset to overwrite all settings.
Deletes a job and all its associated runs. The job is permanently removed and cannot be recovered.
Triggers an immediate run of a job. One run is triggered for each call. Runs triggered by run-now are considered triggered runs. Parameters can be passed to override the job defaults.

params.name: job_id
params.description: The job for which to list runs. If omitted, lists runs for all jobs.

params.name: active_only
params.description: If true, only active runs are included in the results; otherwise lists both active and completed runs.

params.name: completed_only
params.description: If true, only completed runs are included in the results.

params.name: offset
params.description: The offset of the first run to return.

params.name: limit
params.description: The number of runs to return (max 25).

params.name: run_type
params.description: The type of runs to return.

params.name: expand_tasks
params.description: Whether to include task details in each run.

params.name: start_time_from
params.description: Filter runs started after this time (epoch milliseconds).

params.name: start_time_to
params.description: Filter runs started before this time (epoch milliseconds).

items.docs: Lists runs from most recently started to least. Results are paginated. Runs can be filtered by job, active state, and completion status.

params.name: run_id
params.description: The canonical identifier of the run.

params.name: include_history
params.description: Whether to include the repair history in the response.

params.name: include_resolved_values
params.description: Whether to include resolved parameter values in the response.

items.docs: Retrieves the metadata of a run, including start time, end time, status, and task details.
Cancels an active run. The run is canceled asynchronously, and the request returns immediately. The canceled run transitions to a TERMINATING state.

params.name: run_id
params.description: The canonical identifier of the run.

items.docs: Retrieves the output and metadata of a single task run. This endpoint can be used for notebook, JAR, Python, and Spark submit task outputs.

params.name: path
params.description: The absolute path of the workspace directory to list. Must start with /.

params.name: notebooks_modified_after
params.description: If provided, only notebooks modified after this timestamp (epoch seconds) are returned.

items.docs: Lists the contents of a directory in the workspace, or the object if it is not a directory. If the input path does not exist, a RESOURCE_DOES_NOT_EXIST error is returned.

params.name: path
params.description: The absolute path of the workspace object. Must start with /.

items.docs: Gets the status of an object or a directory in the workspace. If the object does not exist, a RESOURCE_DOES_NOT_EXIST error is returned.

params.name: path
params.description: The absolute path of the object or directory to export. Must start with /.

params.name: format
params.description: The format in which to export the notebook. The default is SOURCE.

params.name: direct_download
params.description: Whether to download the file directly. If true, the response is the exported content.

items.docs: Exports a notebook or the contents of an entire directory. Notebooks can be exported in SOURCE, HTML, JUPYTER, or DBC format.
Imports a notebook or the contents of an entire directory. If the object already exists and overwrite is set to false, a RESOURCE_ALREADY_EXISTS error is returned.

Deletes an object or a directory and optionally all of its contents. If the path does not exist, a RESOURCE_DOES_NOT_EXIST error is returned. If path is a non-empty directory and recursive is false, a DIRECTORY_NOT_EMPTY error is returned.

Creates the given directory and necessary parent directories if they do not exist. If there is an object (not a directory) at any prefix of the input path, a RESOURCE_ALREADY_EXISTS error is returned.
